*Before flowroots

---

**

Batch 1 — Cold Outreach — May 2026

'**

Yes — across email, LinkedIn, and in-person conference outreach.

Cold Email — Current HubSpot Sequence:

We have an active 4-email cold outbound sequence in HubSpot currently running. Current stats: 46 contacts enrolled, 17.39% open rate, 0% reply rate, 0 meetings booked. Important context: this sequence was intentionally set up with no links and no graphics to protect our main company domain from deliverability issues — so 0% click rate is expected and not a signal of poor engagement. Emails 3 and 4 have not yet reached any contacts as the sequence is still running. The open rate suggests subject lines and deliverability are working. The gap is conversion from open to reply. Additional finding: Email 1 was 52% personalized but Email 2 dropped to nearly 0% personalized — the sequence loses its personalization after the first touch. Bounce rate is 6.52%, suggesting some contact data quality issues worth addressing in the new list.

Cold Email — Historical:

We have not run structured cold email campaigns or sequences in the past. We sent individual one-off emails to prospects on occasion but without a systematic approach, cadence, or follow-up strategy. Results were minimal — a handful of replies at most. This is effectively our first real cold email campaign.

LinkedIn Outreach:

We have done LinkedIn outreach but not at sufficient volume or with a structured enough approach to draw strong conclusions. Response rates were low overall.

What actually worked — Conference-Based Outreach:

Our strongest pipeline channel to date has been industry conferences. Before attending Advancing Preconstruction, we sent personalized LinkedIn messages to speakers and registered attendees referencing the specific event. Response rate was significantly higher than any cold outreach — many replied and we met them in person at the conference. This worked because it was highly personalized, referenced a specific shared context the prospect cared about, and had a natural low-pressure CTA.

Key takeaways for this campaign:

  

Open rates are achievable with this audience — the problem is body copy and CTA conversion, not subject lines or deliverability

Personalization must be maintained across every touch in the sequence, not just the first email

This audience opens emails but needs a very specific, compelling reason grounded in their real day-to-day pain to reply

Conference and event-based outreach dramatically outperforms cold outreach for this ICP

Plain text emails with no links performed fine on open rate — this audience is not put off by simple formatting

Never lead with product features before establishing that you understand their specific workflow and pain

**''
